verses.text: The LORD tells Moses: go to Pharaoh — let My people go. If you refuse, the hand of the LORD will fall on your cattle in the field, your horses, donkeys, camels, herds, and flocks: a very grievous murrain. But the LORD will sever between Israel's cattle and Egypt's; not one of theirs shall die. Guidance: God increasingly distinguishes whose belongings He protects. The line drawn around Goshen now extends to its animals.

The line drawn around Goshen now extends to its animals.

But the LORD will sever between Israel's cattle and Egypt's; not one of theirs shall die. Guidance: God increasingly distinguishes whose belongings He protects. The LORD tells Moses: go to Pharaoh — let My people go. If you refuse, the hand of the LORD will fall on your cattle in the field, your horses, donkeys, camels, herds, and flocks: a very grievous murrain. But the LORD will sever between Israel's cattle and Egypt's; not one of theirs shall die. Guidance: God increasingly distinguishes whose belongings He protects. The line drawn around Goshen now extends to its animals.

The LORD appointed a set time: tomorrow He will do this thing in the land. The next day all the cattle of Egypt died, but of the cattle of the children of Israel not one. Pharaoh sent and saw it — and yet his heart was hardened. Guidance: Pharaoh investigates the miracle to confirm it is real, then doubles down. Some hearts will examine the evidence carefully and still refuse.

The LORD appointed a set time: tomorrow He will do this thing in the land. The next day all the cattle of Egypt died, but of the cattle of the children of Israel not one. Pharaoh sent and saw it — and yet his heart was hardened. Guidance: Pharaoh investigates the miracle to confirm it is real, then doubles down. Some hearts will examine the evidence carefully and still refuse.

The LORD appointed a set time: tomorrow He will do this thing in the land. The next day all the cattle of Egypt died, but of the cattle of the children of Israel not one. Pharaoh sent and saw it — and yet his heart was hardened. Guidance: Pharaoh investigates the miracle to confirm it is real, then doubles down. Some hearts will examine the evidence carefully and still refuse.

The LORD: take handfuls of ashes from the furnace; let Moses scatter them toward heaven before Pharaoh's eyes. They shall become dust on all Egypt, and boils breaking out with sores on man and beast. They obey; boils break out on every Egyptian. Guidance: God turns the ash of the brick kiln — what Egypt used to break Israel — into the instrument of judgment. Oppression often becomes what answers oppression.

They obey; boils break out on every Egyptian. Guidance: God turns the ash of the brick kiln — what Egypt used to break Israel — into the instrument of judgment. Oppression often becomes what answers oppression.

The LORD: take handfuls of ashes from the furnace; let Moses scatter them toward heaven before Pharaoh's eyes. They shall become dust on all Egypt, and boils breaking out with sores on man and beast. They obey; boils break out on every Egyptian. Guidance: God turns the ash of the brick kiln — what Egypt used to break Israel — into the instrument of judgment. Oppression often becomes what answers oppression.

The magicians could not stand before Moses because of the boils — for the boils were on the magicians and on all the Egyptians. The LORD hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not listen to them, as the LORD had said. Guidance: There is a moment when those who imitated God's signs can no longer even stand. False power is humiliated, but it does not always repent.

The magicians could not stand before Moses because of the boils — for the boils were on the magicians and on all the Egyptians. The LORD hardened Pharaoh's heart, and he did not listen to them, as the LORD had said. Guidance: There is a moment when those who imitated God's signs can no longer even stand. False power is humiliated, but it does not always repent.

The LORD: rise early, stand before Pharaoh — let My people go. This time I will send all My plagues upon your heart, upon your servants, your people, that you may know there is none like Me in all the earth. Guidance: God names the deepest target — your heart. Outward plagues are the loud part; the work He is doing inside you is the real one.

The LORD: rise early, stand before Pharaoh — let My people go. This time I will send all My plagues upon your heart, upon your servants, your people, that you may know there is none like Me in all the earth. Guidance: God names the deepest target — your heart. Outward plagues are the loud part; the work He is doing inside you is the real one.

For now I could have stretched out My hand and struck you and your people with pestilence, and you would have been cut off from the earth. But indeed for this cause I have raised you up — to show in you My power, that My name may be declared throughout all the earth. Guidance: God names His purpose openly. Even resistance has a story arc; Pharaoh's pride is being used to make God's power known to nations he will never meet.

For now I could have stretched out My hand and struck you and your people with pestilence, and you would have been cut off from the earth. But indeed for this cause I have raised you up — to show in you My power, that My name may be declared throughout all the earth. Guidance: God names His purpose openly. Even resistance has a story arc; Pharaoh's pride is being used to make God's power known to nations he will never meet.

Still you exalt yourself against My people, refusing to let them go. Behold, tomorrow about this time I will cause it to rain a very grievous hail, such as has not been in Egypt since its foundation. Therefore send and gather your cattle, all that you have in the field — every man and beast found in the field shall die. Guidance: God warns before He judges. The mercy is in the time given to act. Some warnings come with a window.

Still you exalt yourself against My people, refusing to let them go. Behold, tomorrow about this time I will cause it to rain a very grievous hail, such as has not been in Egypt since its foundation. Still you exalt yourself against My people, refusing to let them go. Behold, tomorrow about this time I will cause it to rain a very grievous hail, such as has not been in Egypt since its foundation. Therefore send and gather your cattle, all that you have in the field — every man and beast found in the field shall die. Guidance: God warns before He judges. The mercy is in the time given to act. Some warnings come with a window.

He who feared the word of the LORD among Pharaoh's servants made his servants and cattle flee into the houses. He who did not regard the word of the LORD left his servants and cattle in the field. Guidance: Even inside Pharaoh's household, fear of God divides people. The same word produces obedience in one heart and indifference in another.

He who feared the word of the LORD among Pharaoh's servants made his servants and cattle flee into the houses. He who did not regard the word of the LORD left his servants and cattle in the field. Guidance: Even inside Pharaoh's household, fear of God divides people. The same word produces obedience in one heart and indifference in another.

The LORD: stretch out your hand toward heaven, that hail may fall on every man, beast, herb in the field of Egypt. Moses stretches his rod; the LORD sends thunder, hail, and fire running along the ground. There has not been hail like it in all Egypt since it became a nation. Guidance: God's judgment is not only severe — it is unprecedented. Sometimes He stages something new precisely so no one can dismiss it as nature taking its course.

The LORD: stretch out your hand toward heaven, that hail may fall on every man, beast, herb in the field of Egypt. Moses stretches his rod; the LORD sends thunder, hail, and fire running along the ground. There has not been hail like it in all Egypt since it became a nation. Guidance: God's judgment is not only severe — it is unprecedented. Sometimes He stages something new precisely so no one can dismiss it as nature taking its course.

The LORD: stretch out your hand toward heaven, that hail may fall on every man, beast, herb in the field of Egypt. Moses stretches his rod; the LORD sends thunder, hail, and fire running along the ground. There has not been hail like it in all Egypt since it became a nation. Guidance: God's judgment is not only severe — it is unprecedented. Sometimes He stages something new precisely so no one can dismiss it as nature taking its course.

The hail strikes every herb of the field, breaks every tree, smites man and beast left outside. Only in the land of Goshen, where the children of Israel are, was there no hail. Guidance: The same sky was over both lands. The difference was God's hand. His protection of His people is not always visible until you look at what is happening next door.

The hail strikes every herb of the field, breaks every tree, smites man and beast left outside. Only in the land of Goshen, where the children of Israel are, was there no hail. Guidance: The same sky was over both lands. The difference was God's hand. His protection of His people is not always visible until you look at what is happening next door.

Pharaoh sends for Moses and Aaron: I have sinned this time. The LORD is righteous; I and my people are wicked. Entreat the LORD — enough mighty thunderings and hail — I will let you go. Guidance: This is Pharaoh's first confession. It sounds real. But confession under duress is not the same as repentance. Watch what he does once the rain stops.

Pharaoh sends for Moses and Aaron: I have sinned this time. The LORD is righteous; I and my people are wicked. Entreat the LORD — enough mighty thunderings and hail — I will let you go. Guidance: This is Pharaoh's first confession. It sounds real. But confession under duress is not the same as repentance. Watch what he does once the rain stops.

Moses: when I am out of the city I will spread my hands to the LORD; the thunder will cease and there will be no more hail — that you may know the earth is the LORD's. But as for you and your servants, I know that you will not yet fear the LORD God. Guidance: Even God's deliverer can see clearly what a heart will do. Moses prays anyway.

Moses: when I am out of the city I will spread my hands to the LORD; the thunder will cease and there will be no more hail — that you may know the earth is the LORD's. But as for you and your servants, I know that you will not yet fear the LORD God. Guidance: Even God's deliverer can see clearly what a heart will do. Moses prays anyway.

The flax and the barley were struck, for the barley was in the ear and the flax was in bloom. But the wheat and the rye were not struck, for they were not yet grown. Guidance: The text records exactly which crops were ruined and which still stood. God's judgments are precise; He spares what He chooses to spare.

The flax and the barley were struck, for the barley was in the ear and the flax was in bloom. But the wheat and the rye were not struck, for they were not yet grown. Guidance: The text records exactly which crops were ruined and which still stood. God's judgments are precise; He spares what He chooses to spare.

Moses goes out, spreads his hands, and the thunder and hail cease; the rain is not poured on the earth. But when Pharaoh sees the rain stop, he sins yet more and hardens his heart — he and his servants. He would not let Israel go, as the LORD had spoken. Guidance: Confession in the storm does not always survive the sunshine. Hardening is a habit; each time it gets easier.
